acceptance.proof: Public Rust constructors make metadata collection and text bounds explicit, return typed deterministic errors for exceeded bounds, and never expose partially accepted state.
A dependency-free sample value validates its declared channel count before construction and preserves the caller-provided values without runtime side effects.
Focused tests cover exact-limit success, one-past-limit rejection, malformed limits, channel-count mismatch, and stable error payloads aligned with the accepted STRM-000 contract cases.
The crate remains one unpublished std-only package with no dependencies, features, unsafe code, runtime effects, protocol implementation, or compatibility overclaim.

commit_policy: Commit the accepted bounded contract slice separately from protocol, runtime, networking, or adapter work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the first implemented contract slice while keeping protocol, runtime, and authority boundaries closed.
Describe the exact local contract behavior now implemented without promoting it to LSL interoperability.
Review whether the bounded contract and evidence split exposes any reusable system-engineering rule; avoid a repo-specific recipe when existing guidance is sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: XML parsing or serialization, LSL protocol constants, wire framing or encoding, discovery, networking, sockets, process spawning, or background threads.
Clock correction, timestamp smoothing, inlet, outlet, buffering, queueing, recovery, provider selection, FFI, native-library loading, or language bindings.
Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, additional crates, generated protocol material, copied or translated liblsl or rLSL source, and production liblsl integration.
Morphospace adapters, Manifold admission or stream authority, platform lifecycle, Quest or Hostess behavior, device validation, and any wire or ecosystem compatibility claim.
objective: Implement the first dependency-free Rust contract slice for bounded metadata and sample-shape validation without opening any LSL protocol or runtime surface.
outputs: Dependency-free bounded metadata and sample-shape Rust APIs with typed errors.
Positive and damaged unit tests tied to the accepted STRM-000 contract case identifiers.
Deterministic CORE-001 validation entrypoint and updated source-only gate.
Exact public status and instruction updates that do not claim protocol, wire, runtime, or ecosystem support.
